title: [sha512] image: add configurable digest support
body:
<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->


Depends on https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/374 . This includes the commit from there, but maybe better to let that one go in first and then I can rebase this one.

(cursor assisted)

comments.body: ✅ A new PR has been created in buildah to vendor these changes: **https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6412**
@mtrmac I think I've addressed everything so far along with the requested change. Also, rebased on the latest in #374 as well. PTAL. Thanks a lot for all the detailed comments.
@mtrmac This is intended at not before podman v5.8, so not in an immediate rush. I'll be happy to fix the storage stuff before we go forward with this, if you prefer.

@mtrmac yup, I'll clean out the storage stuff first and then get to this one. A lot of the AI assistance ended up being just added pain. I'll clean out the slop here too after storage and ping you once that's done. Thanks for the reviews so far.

@lsm5 just to potentially avoid duplication / share what I’m thinking:

Given that
- I also have a goal to experiment with AI
- I don’t trust LLMs to _reliably_ work on large codebases (the randomness inherent in "temperature" means that mistakes will eventually happen)
- reviewing a LLM-based “audit” is ~equivalent to doing that audit myself

I’m toying with https://github.com/mtrmac/container-libs/tree/digest-audit . (The commit messages are LLM-generated and not worth reading), an AI-written but carefully human-reviewed _tool_ to identify all relevant uses. Cursor went down just as it was getting interesting, but I think this has potential: unlike a regular grep, we should be able to _reliably_ filter out all of the digest value / parameter / field passing around, and things like `digest.String()` _in an error message_, leaving us with only the exact set of Go code that is _doing something_ with it (calling `.Encoded`, comparisons of two digests, map keys, …). That should result in a much more manageable to-do list.

And then, maybe, we can find and handle “the easy” but nevertheless blocking stuff like “validate blob against a digest”, making it more feasible to think about the harder parts that need designing.

@lsm5 I think https://github.com/mtrmac/container-libs/tree/digest-audit , as of 92e086a407559ddd5d2b889aa910356a1eca232a , might be broadly useful. (It’s by no means _good_ code, e.g. most of the hard-coded logic should be a config file.). It detects all _non-trivial_ uses of `digest.Digest` or `digest.Algorithm` (ignoring copies and https://github.com/mtrmac/container-libs/blob/92e086a407559ddd5d2b889aa910356a1eca232a/digest-audit/main.go#L238 .

E.g.
```console
% go run ./digest-audit ./image/copy     
image/copy/blob.go:134:33: binary-op !=
image/copy/compression.go:375:29: binary-op !=
image/copy/digesting_reader.go:28:36: selector Algorithm
image/copy/digesting_reader.go:29:22: selector Available
image/copy/digesting_reader.go:32:29: selector Digester
image/copy/digesting_reader.go:55:6: binary-op !=
image/copy/progress_bars.go:65:59: selector Encoded
image/copy/single.go:406:5: binary-op !=
image/copy/single.go:566:10: binary-op ==
image/copy/single.go:654:6: binary-op !=
image/copy/single.go:915:5: binary-op ==
image/copy/single.go:990:26: selector FromReader
```

It’s _by no means_ the case that everything reported is incorrect — e.g. the correct way to match digests by computing `referenceDigest.Algorithm().FromBytes() == referenceDigest` will trigger at least three matches — but it _should_ highlight where to look, e.g. all the locations where we directly compare, or use as map keys.

There’s certainly more that can be automatically filtered out.
